header: 2A Theoretical Framework for Robust and Distortion-Free Watermarking

images.caption: Figure 2:Overview of PASA.Left:Construction of the semantic mapping functionff, which partitions the latent token embedding space intoKKsemantic clusters.Right: Top (Generation).(G1) At each steptt, the NTP distributionQtQ_{t}is transformed into the cluster distributionQtfQ_{t}^{f}. (G2) The auxiliary distributionPζtP_{\zeta_{t}}is truncated by a thresholdα\alphaand contains an overflow stateζ~\tilde{\zeta}to ensure FA error control. (G3) Auxiliary sampling ofζt\zeta_{t}uses a𝗌𝖾𝖾𝖽t\mathsf{seed}_{t}generated by a PRF with a secret key andwwsemantic history as input. (G4) The sampled auxiliary random variableζt\zeta_{t}guides the sampling of the next tokenxtx_{t}within the selected semantic cluster.Bottom (Detection).(D0-D2) For a potentially modified observed token sequence, the detector approximates the generation distribution through an SLM. (D3) The detection score accumulates based on the alignment between the resampledζt\zeta_{t}and the observed semantic clusterf​(xt)f(x_{t}).

Figure 3:Ablation study on hyper-parameters.(a) Impact of semantic cluster granularity (KK) on robustness across log-scale cluster counts. (b) Impact of synchronization window size (ww) on robustness. The plots compare the baseline (Original) against T5-based token replacement attacks (r=0.3,0.5r=0.3,0.5).
Figure 4:Detection performance across various generated text lengths.The ROC-AUC and True Positive Rate (TPR) exhibit rapid convergence, achieving near-perfect detection beyond 300 tokens.
